questions.question: What are the main differences between supervised and unsupervised learning? Provide examples of each.
expectedPoints: Supervised learning uses labeled data with known outcomes
Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data
Example of supervised: spam classification
Example of unsupervised: customer segmentation
Supervised requires human-labeled data, unsupervised discovers structure automatically

questions.question: Describe the machine learning process with a detailed explanation of each step.
expectedPoints: Problem definition: Understanding business objectives and formulating as ML problem
Data collection: Gathering relevant data from various sources
Data preparation: Cleaning, transforming, and engineering features
Model selection: Choosing appropriate algorithms for the problem
Model training: Using training data to build the model
Model evaluation: Assessing performance using validation and test sets
Model deployment: Integrating the model into production systems
Model maintenance: Monitoring performance and updating as needed

questions.question: What is overfitting and how can it be prevented?
expectedPoints: Overfitting: Model learns training data too well, including noise
Poor performance on new, unseen data
Prevention methods: Cross-validation, regularization, simplifying model
Early stopping, increasing training data, feature selection

questions.question: Explain three real-world applications of machine learning in different industries.
expectedPoints: Healthcare: Disease diagnosis from medical images, drug discovery
Finance: Fraud detection, algorithmic trading, credit scoring
Retail: Recommendation systems, demand forecasting, price optimization
Manufacturing: Predictive maintenance, quality control, process optimization
Transportation: Autonomous vehicles, route optimization, traffic prediction

questions.question: What are the main challenges in machine learning projects?
expectedPoints: Data quality issues: missing values, noise, inconsistencies
Data quantity: insufficient training data
Overfitting and underfitting problems
Computational complexity and resource requirements
Model interpretability and explainability
Deployment and integration challenges
